name: Fluent UI
description: Microsoft's official React component library implementing Fluent 2 design system for building Microsoft 365-like experiences

description.value: Microsoft's official React component library implementing Fluent 2 design system for building Microsoft 365-like experiences

license.comment: MIT license for code; separate assets license for fonts and icons

active-maintenance.comment: Very active - v9.72.8 released December 2025; maintained by Microsoft with full-time team; used in Microsoft 365, Office, and Edge

initial-release.comment: Repository created June 6, 2016; originally Office UI Fabric, renamed to Fluent UI in 2020

framework.comment: Available for both React (v9) and Web Components; also has separate React Native library (@fluentui/react-native)

styling-approach.comment: Uses Griffel, Microsoft's open-source CSS-in-JS with near-zero runtime and optional build-time transforms

component-model.comment: Fully styled components implementing Fluent 2 design system

typescript.comment: Written in TypeScript (94.9% of codebase) with first-class type definitions

ssr-compatible.comment: Traditional SSR supported via Griffel's SSR capabilities; requires SSRProvider setup for Next.js Pages Router

rsc-compatible.comment: React Server Components NOT supported due to heavy reliance on React Context and client-side Griffel CSS-in-JS; must use 'use client' directive

design-system.comment: Implements Microsoft's Fluent 2 design system; designed for Microsoft 365 and Office integration

dark-mode.comment: Built-in light and dark theme support with localStorage detection for user preferences

theming-system.comment: Comprehensive theming via FluentProvider with webLightTheme, webDarkTheme, and custom theme support

css-variables.comment: Fluent 2 design language uses CSS variables for color, stroke width, corner radius, spacing, and size tokens

design-tokens.comment: Global and alias design tokens for color, stroke width, corner radius, spacing, and size; tokens map between design and code

rtl-support.comment: Automatic RTL support via FluentProvider dir prop; CSS is automatically flipped using rtl-css-js library

component-count.comment: 60 components listed in Fluent 2 design system documentation

text-input.comment: Input component for standard text input

textarea.comment: Textarea component for multi-line text input

select.comment: Select component (also called Dropdown in v8) for single selection

multi-select.comment: Combobox and TagPicker components support multiple selection

combobox.comment: Combobox component for searchable/filterable selection with autocomplete

checkbox.comment: Checkbox component for single and grouped checkboxes

radio.comment: Radio Group component for mutually exclusive selections

switch-toggle.comment: Switch component for boolean toggle values; v9.72.8 added small variant

number-input.comment: Spin Button component for numeric input with increment/decrement controls

tag-input.comment: Tag Picker component for multiple tag/token input with add/remove functionality

pin-input.comment: No dedicated OTP/PIN input component with individual digit boxes

color-picker.comment: No built-in color picker component in v9

date-picker.comment: Date Picker component available; previously in v8, now in v9

time-picker.comment: Time Picker component for time selection

date-range-picker.comment: No dedicated date range picker component in v9

dropzone.comment: No built-in drag-and-drop file upload area

input-groups.comment: Input component supports content before/after slots for addons

input-with-dropdown.comment: Searchbox component combines input with dropdown functionality

button-groups.comment: Toolbar component can be used for grouped buttons

split-button.comment: Menu component with MenuSplitGroup for split button with dropdown

table.comment: Table component for basic data display

data-table.comment: DataGrid component with sorting, selection, and navigation; built on Table primitives

virtual-infinite-list.comment: VirtualizedList component in v9 for virtualized scrolling

tree-view.comment: Tree component for hierarchical expandable tree structure

card.comment: Card component with CardHeader, CardPreview, CardFooter subcomponents

avatar.comment: Avatar component showing image or text to represent a person or group

badge.comment: Badge component as visual indicator for status; also PresenceBadge and CounterBadge variants

tag-chip-pill.comment: Tag component for standalone labels; includes InteractionTag for interactive use

timeline.comment: No dedicated timeline component in v9

calendar.comment: No standalone calendar display component; DatePicker includes calendar for selection only

alert.comment: Message Bar component for inline messages (info, warning, error, success)

toast-notification.comment: Toast component for temporary popup notifications

progress-bar.comment: Progress Bar component for linear progress indication

progress-circle.comment: No circular progress indicator component; Spinner provides indeterminate circular loading

spinner-loader.comment: Spinner component for indeterminate loading indication

skeleton.comment: Skeleton component for content placeholders during loading

modal-dialog.comment: Dialog component for modal overlays with focus trapping

drawer-sheet.comment: Drawer component for slide-in panels; v9.72.8 added support to keep mounted on close

popover.comment: Popover component for floating content anchored to trigger

dropdown-menu.comment: Menu component for action menus with keyboard navigation

context-menu.comment: Menu component can be triggered by right-click for context menu functionality

command-palette.comment: No built-in keyboard-driven command palette component

tabs.comment: TabList component for tab-based content switching

breadcrumb.comment: Breadcrumb component for hierarchical path navigation

pagination.comment: No dedicated pagination component in v9

stepper.comment: No dedicated stepper/wizard component in v9

navigation-menu.comment: Nav component for site navigation with nested items

sidebar.comment: No dedicated sidebar component; Nav and Drawer can be used to build sidebars

bottom-navigation.comment: No dedicated mobile-style bottom navigation bar

grid.comment: No built-in CSS grid layout component; use native CSS or other layout solutions

stack.comment: No Stack component in v9; available in v8 but not carried forward

divider.comment: Divider component for horizontal/vertical separators; v9.72.8 added headless style hook

collapsible.comment: Accordion component for expandable/collapsible content sections

rich-text-editors.comment: No official rich text editor integration

code-editors.comment: Monaco Editor (VS Code's editor) is a separate Microsoft project commonly used with Fluent UI but no official integration

chart-libraries.comment: @fluentui/react-charting available but v8-based; migration to v9 in progress; not fully compatible with v9 theme tokens yet

form-libraries.comment: Community integrations with React Hook Form and Formik; Fluent UI uses Field component for form layout

animation-libraries.comment: Built-in motion APIs via createPresenceComponent() and createMotionComponent()

icon-libraries.comment: @fluentui/react-icons provides Fluent System Icons as React components; SVG-based with Filled and Regular variants

date-libraries.comment: No documented official date library integration; date components handle dates internally

aria-compliant.comment: Components follow WAI-ARIA patterns; extensive accessibility review checklist for all components

keyboard-navigation.comment: Full keyboard navigation with equivalent efficiency to mouse interactions; follows ARIA Authoring Practices

screen-reader-tested.comment: Tested with JAWS, NVDA, Narrator on Windows; VoiceOver on macOS/iOS; TalkBack on Android

focus-management.comment: Proper focus management with focus trapping in dialogs and modals; follows established patterns

wcag-level.comment: Built to meet WCAG 2.1 specifications; each component goes through extensive manual accessibility test pass

accessibility-rating.comment: Excellent - WCAG 2.1 compliant, tested by trusted testers across multiple screen readers and browsers, High Contrast mode support, comprehensive a11y review process

bundle-size-full.comment: Full bundle size varies; @fluentui/react-components is a suite package with tree-shakeable components

bundle-size-core.comment: Tree-shakeable architecture; bundle size depends on components imported

tree-shakeable.comment: Components are individually importable for tree-shaking; Griffel CSS-in-JS supports build-time transforms

zero-runtime.comment: Griffel provides 'near zero runtime' with SSR and build-time transforms, but still has some runtime overhead

lazy-loading.comment: Components can be individually imported and lazy loaded

documentation-quality.comment: Good documentation with Storybook-based examples, API references, and Fluent 2 design system docs; some gaps in migration guides between v8/v9

interactive-docs.comment: Storybook-based documentation with interactive component playground

storybook.comment: Official Storybook available at storybooks.fluentui.dev; includes accessibility testing addon

figma-kit.comment: Official Microsoft Fluent 2 Web UI kit on Figma Community; component properties map to code; includes variables for color, spacing, radius, etc.

cli-generator.comment: No CLI tool for scaffolding components

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: 145 projects using @fluentui/react-components; exact weekly downloads not available from search

commercial-support.comment: Backed by Microsoft with full-time development team; enterprise-grade support through Microsoft's ecosystem

style-override.comment: Customization via className, style props, CSS variables, and Griffel's makeStyles API

compound-components.comment: Extensive compound component pattern (e.g., Card.Header, Menu.Item, Accordion.Header, etc.)

render-props.comment: Primarily uses slots and compound components rather than render props

slots.comment: Components use slots pattern for content injection; properties in UI kits map to slots in code

unstyled-mode.comment: Fluent UI v9 is styled; for headless primitives, use separate libraries like Radix or React Aria
